meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Analytics Setup to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Stop guessing why your code fails. Learn to implement robust analytics to debug faster with this beginner-friendly guide using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Debugging blind is the primary cause of freelancer burnout. By integrating event-based analytics, you transform 'unknown errors' into actionable data streams. Tracking user interactions and state changes allows you to pinpoint exactly where your application logic breaks, turning hours of tedious investigation into seconds of precision fixing.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, the clock is ticking, and a client is expecting a live feature. The code works on your machine, but in production, it’s a black box of silent failures. This is the 'Freelancer’s Nightmare'—you have no visibility into what happens when the user clicks that button.

Stop relying on guesswork. By instrumenting your application with strategic analytics, you gain the eyes required to see every failure point in real-time. This guide replaces frantic console logging with professional-grade observability, ensuring you ship faster and spend significantly less time playing detective.

steps.title: Define Your Critical Event Map
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the top 5 user actions that, if they fail, constitute a 'critical bug'. This focuses your debugging efforts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest 5 critical user action events that should be tracked to monitor for potential runtime errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Analyze my main application flow and define 5 critical tracking events for observability.'

steps.title: Inject Analytics Middleware
steps.description: Create a centralized wrapper for your API calls and state updates to ensure every event captures context like user ID and timestamp.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust TypeScript middleware function that wraps API calls to log success/failure events automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Create a central analytics wrapper for all outgoing API requests.'

steps.title: Automate Error Boundary Logging
steps.description: Standardize your error boundaries so they automatically fire an analytics event with the stack trace whenever a component crashes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my existing error boundaries to push error stack traces to an external analytics endpoint on catch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Update ErrorBoundary.tsx to include an analytics event push on catch.'

steps.title: Implement User-Session Context
steps.description: Attach session metadata to every event so you can correlate individual bugs with specific browser environments or user configurations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a hook that attaches browser version, OS, and session ID to every tracked analytics event.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Add a custom hook that injects session metadata into all analytics calls.'

steps.title: Validate Event Delivery
steps.description: Use a testing suite to ensure that your analytics events actually fire before you deploy to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a unit test for my analytics service that verifies events are triggered correctly during a simulated user flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /test 'Write unit tests for the analytics tracking service.'

pro_tips: Never log PII (Personally Identifiable Information) in your analytics payload to maintain GDPR compliance.
Use sampling for high-frequency events to avoid hitting rate limits and incurring unnecessary infrastructure costs.
Always set a 'fallback' for your analytics service so that if the analytics provider goes down, your main application logic remains untouched.
Tag your events with the current deployment version to quickly identify if a bug was introduced in a specific release.
Configure alerts for 'empty' events, which often signal that a tracking script was blocked by an ad-blocker.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, set up a dashboard to visualize your error rates. Monitor these for 48 hours to establish a baseline, then start optimizing the most frequent failure points.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start mastering your environment. Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
